title: Move item BACK to original board after status changes
excerpt:

posts.author: fvnnel_jari (Jari)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!
I’m trying to set up an automation where I can move an item to another board (say, for ‘Review’). Which is all well and good and there is enough information to figure out. But what I can’t find is how to move that item BACK to it’s original board (And setting up the same automation doesn’t work, since the items are from separate boards), when the status is again changed (from ‘review’ to ‘working on it’)
Is there anything anyone can recommend to accomplish this?

posts.author: GiannisKoukounas (Giannis Koukounas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @fvnnel_jari ,

Welcome to the community !

You would need an additional identifier to know in which board the item needs to move back to.

That way, you would need to set up automations like this one:

image1179×379 18.6 KB

Let me know if this helps!

Best Regards,
Giannis, Implementation Consultant at thespelas.com

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @fvnnel_jari ,

you can accomplish this by creating a automation ,when the status change to something (from ‘review’ to ‘working on it’) move item to the original board.

Hope its helpful for u.

MSquare Support
Visit us here
Youtube Channel

posts.author: fvnnel_jari (Jari)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks, @GiannisKoukounas , But how do I create that ‘IF’ statement. I can’t find the option to be able to define the original board.

posts.author: fvnnel_jari (Jari)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there an automation for setting the original board?

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there a way to do this without creating a new item in the original board, but just “reactivating it” by changing the status there?

posts.author: GiannisKoukounas (Giannis Koukounas)
posts.cookedHtml: I am not 100% certain on what you mean, can you please explain further? Thanks!

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: How do you know what the original board was?
